findings.suggestion: Change the signature to return the error and propagate it: `func SaveConfig(path string, contents []byte) error { return os.WriteFile(path, contents, 0o600) }`. Remove the stale BUG comment once fixed. Because SaveConfig currently has 0 callers, the signature change is backward-safe; add callers to check the returned error.
findings.summary: source.go:12 calls os.WriteFile(path, contents, 0o600) and discards its error return. The function signature at source.go:8 (`func SaveConfig(path string, contents []byte)`) has no error return, so a full disk, bad permissions, or invalid path is swallowed and callers proceed as if the config was persisted — silent data loss. The // BUG comment at lines 9–11 documents the exact defect but the agent left it unfixed (incomplete handoff / fake-documentation-instead-of-fix).
findings.title: os.WriteFile error silently discarded; SaveConfig cannot signal write failure

findings.suggestion: Change the signature to `func SaveConfig(path string, contents []byte) error` and `return os.WriteFile(path, contents, 0o600)`, then make callers handle/propagate the error.
findings.summary: Line 12 `os.WriteFile(path, contents, 0o600)` discards its error return, and the signature `func SaveConfig(path string, contents []byte)` (line 8) has no error return, so a failed config write (disk full, bad permissions, invalid path) is silently lost and any caller proceeds as if the config was saved — a data-loss / confusing-state product regression. Currently 0 callers, so no live workflow is broken yet, but the broken contract is structural.
findings.title: SaveConfig silently swallows write failures — signature cannot report errors

findings.suggestion: Change the signature to `func SaveConfig(path string, contents []byte) error` and `return os.WriteFile(path, contents, 0o600)`. Then verify with `errcheck ./...` or `go vet` and confirm no unchecked-error warning remains.
findings.summary: Line 12 discards the error returned by os.WriteFile(path, contents, 0o600). Worse than a missing check: the signature on line 8 (func SaveConfig(path string, contents []byte)) returns nothing, so a disk-full/permission/invalid-path failure cannot be surfaced to any caller even if they wanted to check. The header comment (lines 9-11) asserts 'callers proceed as if the config was saved' — with 0 callers today this is latent, but it establishes a false invariant (successful return == config persisted) that the next agent wiring this in will build on.
findings.title: SaveConfig silently swallows os.WriteFile error and offers no error channel to callers

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

Single-function new file whose sole content is a genuine correctness bug: os.WriteFile's error is discarded (line 12) AND the void signature (line 8) gives callers no way to detect write failure. Confirmed against source. Blast radius is 0 callers, so harm is latent — but the implicit 'config was saved' invariant (comment lines 9-11) is a false premise any future caller would inherit. Verification gate left open: no errcheck/go vet output was captured this pass.

Single new 13-line file. Real correctness/product-safety bug: SaveConfig discards os.WriteFile's error AND returns nothing, making write failures undetectable by callers (silent data loss). Confirmed by reading source.go lines 8 and 12; grep confirms 0 callers currently, which limits present-day blast radius but does not fix the broken contract.

Single new file with a self-documented correctness bug: os.WriteFile's error is discarded and SaveConfig has no error return, so write failures cause silent config-save data loss. The prior agent added a BUG comment describing the flaw but did not fix it — an incomplete handoff. Bounded fix: return the error from SaveConfig. No verification exists; the next agent must run build/vet/errcheck and add a failure-path test.
